title: Wegovy Weight-Loss Pills Approved for Use in the UK
subtitle: A new tablet version of the popular weight-loss drug offers a different option for patients, but experts warn it is not a simple solution.

summary.en: The UK medicines regulator has officially approved a pill version of the weight-loss drug Wegovy. This is the first time a tablet form of this medication has been cleared for use in the country. While the drug is already known for helping people lose weight by suppressing appetite, the new pill offers a more convenient option for some patients. However, the drug will not be available on the NHS until experts finish assessing it. Health officials emphasize that the medication must be used alongside a healthy diet and exercise.
summary.zh: 英國藥品監管機構已正式核准減肥藥物 Wegovy 的口服錠劑版本，這是該藥物首次以錠劑形式獲准在英國使用。雖然該藥物以抑制食慾的減重效果聞名，但新推出的錠劑為部分病患提供了更便利的選擇。不過，在專家完成評估前，該藥物尚無法透過國民保健署取得。衛生官員強調，此藥物必須搭配健康飲食與運動才能發揮效果。

paragraphs.en: The UK medicines regulator, the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA), has officially approved a new pill version of the weight-loss drug Wegovy. This development marks the first time that a tablet form of this specific medication has been cleared for use in the country. The decision makes the UK the third nation to authorize the pills, following the United States and the United Arab Emirates.
paragraphs.zh: 英國藥品監管機構「藥品與保健產品管理局」（MHRA）已正式核准減肥藥物「Wegovy」的口服錠劑版本。這是該藥物首次以錠劑形式獲准在英國使用。此項決定使英國成為繼美國與阿拉伯聯合大公國之後，第三個核准該藥物的國家。

paragraphs.en: Wegovy contains a substance called semaglutide. This works by mimicking a natural hormone in the body known as GLP-1, which helps to suppress appetite and slows down digestion. By doing so, it helps people feel fuller for longer periods. According to the BBC, the manufacturer, Novo Nordisk, stated that the once-a-day tablet could be more convenient for many patients than the current weekly injections. Furthermore, unlike the injectable version, the pills do not need to be kept in a refrigerator.
paragraphs.zh: Wegovy 含有名為「司美格魯肽」（semaglutide）的成分。其作用原理是模擬人體內一種稱為「升糖素類似胜肽-1」（GLP-1）的天然荷爾蒙，藉此抑制食慾並減緩消化速度，幫助使用者產生更持久的飽足感。根據 BBC 報導，製造商諾和諾德（Novo Nordisk）表示，對於許多病患而言，每日服用一次的錠劑可能比目前的每週注射更為方便。此外，與注射劑型不同，這些藥錠無需冷藏保存。

paragraphs.en: While the approval is a significant step, the drug will not be available through the National Health Service (NHS) immediately. The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (Nice) must first assess the drug to decide if it should be recommended for general use. According to the BBC, while Nice has not yet been formally approached by the manufacturer, the two parties are currently in active dialogue. For now, patients who wish to use the drug will need to obtain a private prescription from pharmacies.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管獲得核准是重要的一步，但該藥物目前還無法透過英國國民保健署（NHS）取得。英國國家健康與照顧卓越研究院（Nice）必須先進行評估，以決定是否建議將其納入一般醫療用途。根據 BBC 報導，雖然製造商尚未正式向該研究院提出申請，但雙方目前正積極對話中。現階段，有意使用該藥物的病患必須透過藥局取得私人處方。

paragraphs.en: Clinical trials have shown that patients on the highest dose of the medication lost between 14% and 17% of their body weight after 64 weeks. The MHRA has set clear guidelines for how the drug should be used. Patients will start on a 1.5 mg dose, which will gradually increase to 4 mg, 9 mg, and finally 25 mg over several months. Those who are already using the 2.4 mg weekly injection may be able to switch directly to the 25 mg daily tablet, provided their doctor agrees it is suitable.
paragraphs.zh: 臨床試驗顯示，服用最高劑量藥物的病患在 64 週後體重減輕了 14% 至 17%。MHRA 已針對該藥物的使用方式制定了明確指引。病患將從 1.5 毫克劑量開始，並在數月內逐步增加至 4 毫克、9 毫克，最終達到 25 毫克。若醫師評估合適，目前已在使用每週 2.4 毫克注射劑的病患，或許能直接轉換為每日 25 毫克的錠劑。

paragraphs.en: Despite the excitement surrounding the new form of the drug, medical experts are urging caution. Prof. Victoria Tzortziou Brown, president of the Royal College of GPs, noted that while the tablet form is easier to take than an injection, it should not be viewed as a "magic solution" for weight loss. She emphasized that the medication comes with potential side effects and is not appropriate for everyone. Common issues reported by users include nausea, vomiting, constipation, and diarrhea. The MHRA advises that anyone experiencing these side effects should report them to their doctor or through the official yellow card scheme.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管大眾對此新劑型感到興奮，醫學專家仍呼籲應保持謹慎。英國皇家全科醫師學會（Royal College of GPs）主席維多利亞·佐齊歐·布朗（Victoria Tzortziou Brown）教授指出，雖然錠劑比注射劑更容易服用，但不應將其視為減肥的「神奇解方」。她強調，該藥物伴隨潛在副作用，並非適合所有人。使用者常見的症狀包括噁心、嘔吐、便秘與腹瀉。MHRA 建議，若出現這些副作用，應向醫師報告，或透過官方的「黃卡計畫」（yellow card scheme）進行通報。

paragraphs.en: Furthermore, health professionals stress that the medication is intended for specific groups, such as adults who are obese or those who are overweight and have at least one weight-related health condition. It is not meant for short-term or cosmetic weight loss. Experts strongly advise against using these drugs as a quick fix to get ready for specific events. Instead, the medication should be part of a broader approach to health, which includes a balanced diet, regular physical activity, and behavioral support.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，醫療專業人員強調，該藥物僅適用於特定族群，例如肥胖成人，或是過重且至少患有一種與體重相關疾病的患者。它並非用於短期或美容目的的減重。專家強烈建議不要將這些藥物視為為了特定活動而快速瘦身的捷徑。相反地，藥物應作為整體健康計畫的一部分，其中必須包含均衡飲食、規律運動與行為支持。

paragraphs.en: Olivier Picard, the chair of the National Pharmacy Association, called the approval "significant" and noted that it could help many patients who are unable or unwilling to use the injectable version. He added that pharmacies are currently waiting for further guidance on when the treatment will be available to the public. While the exact cost in the UK is not yet known, patients hope that the tablet form might eventually reduce the cost of treatment compared to the current private prices for injections, which can range from £90 to £300 per month.
paragraphs.zh: 英國國家藥局協會（National Pharmacy Association）主席奧利維耶·皮卡德（Olivier Picard）稱此核准為「重大進展」，並指出這能幫助許多無法或不願使用注射劑型的病患。他補充說，藥局目前正等待關於該療法何時能對大眾開放的進一步指引。雖然目前在英國的確切費用尚未公布，但病患希望錠劑能降低治療成本，相較於目前每月約 90 至 300 英鎊的私人注射劑費用，或許能更為親民。

paragraphs.en: As the UK joins other nations in offering this new treatment option, the focus remains on ensuring it is used safely. The long-term success of the medication depends on the patient's commitment to lifestyle changes. As noted by the BBC, there is always a risk that weight may return after a patient stops taking the medication. Therefore, medical professionals continue to emphasize that the drug is a tool to support, not replace, healthy habits.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著英國加入其他國家的行列提供此項新治療選擇，重點仍在於確保其使用安全。該藥物的長期成功取決於病患對生活型態改變的堅持。正如 BBC 所述，病患停藥後體重回升的風險始終存在。因此，醫療專業人員持續強調，該藥物是用來輔助而非取代健康習慣的工具。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Officially approved or authorized for use.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「清除」或「清理」，這裡指藥物通過審核。文中：This development marks the first time that a tablet form of this specific medication has been cleared for use in the country.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A quick or easy solution to a problem.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞「修理」，這裡作名詞，指快速解決問題的方法。文中：Experts strongly advise against using these drugs as a quick fix to get ready for specific events.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A formal discussion or exchange of views between two parties.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於文學或戲劇中的「對白」，這裡指機構間的正式協商。文中：According to the BBC, while Nice has not yet been formally approached by the manufacturer, the two parties are currently in active dialogue.

previousArticles.title: Wegovy Weight-Loss Pills Approved for Use in the UK
previousArticles.summaryEn: The UK medicines regulator has approved a pill version of the weight-loss drug Wegovy. This marks the first time a tablet form of this GLP-1 medication has been cleared for use in the country. While the drug is known for helping people lose weight by suppressing appetite, it is not yet available on the NHS. Experts emphasize that the medication should be part of a broader health plan including diet and exercise. The approval follows recent discussions about the wide-ranging potential benefits of GLP-1 drugs for various health conditions.

previousArticles.title: New Drug Offers Hope for Pancreatic Cancer Patients
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have shared positive news regarding a new drug for pancreatic cancer. The drug, called daraxonrasib, has shown the ability to double survival times in patients. Experts describe the development as a significant clinical breakthrough. However, doctors warn that the disease remains very dangerous and difficult to treat. Further research continues as scientists look for ways to improve long-term outcomes.

previousArticles.title: Doctors Release New Vaccine Guidelines for Pregnant Women
previousArticles.summaryEn: The American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) has released its own official vaccine schedule for the first time. This decision follows recent changes to government health advice that caused confusion among patients and doctors. The new guidelines recommend vaccines for COVID-19 and the flu, which are no longer included in official government recommendations. ACOG stated that this move is necessary to provide clear, evidence-based information to the public. Thirteen other medical organizations have joined ACOG in supporting these new recommendations.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is one specific requirement for a patient to switch from the weekly injection to the daily tablet?

options.A: They must have been using the injection for at least 64 weeks.
options.B: They must receive approval from their doctor that the switch is appropriate.
options.C: They must have already reached the maximum 25 mg dose of the injection.
options.D: They must be currently registered with the National Health Service (NHS).

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，文中明確提到那些正在使用 2.4 mg 每週注射劑的患者，若要切換到 25 mg 的每日藥丸，前提是必須經過醫生的評估與同意（provided their doctor agrees it is suitable）。選項 A 錯誤，因為 64 週是臨床試驗的時間，而非切換條件；選項 C 錯誤，因為注射劑與藥丸的劑量單位不同，文中並未要求患者必須先達到 25 mg 注射劑量；選項 D 錯誤，因為目前該藥物尚未進入 NHS 體系，患者需透過私人處方取得。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current availability of the Wegovy pill in the UK?

options.A: It is currently being distributed to all pharmacies via the NHS.
options.B: It is cheaper than the injectable version of the drug.
options.C: It is legally approved for use but not yet accessible through public health funding.
options.D: It is only available to patients who are willing to participate in clinical trials.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段，MHRA 雖然已經核准了該藥物，但 Nice（國家健康與護理卓越研究院）尚未將其納入 NHS 的推薦清單，因此目前患者只能透過私人處方購買。選項 A 錯誤，因為它尚未透過 NHS 提供；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到價格尚未確定，僅是患者「希望」未來能變便宜；選項 D 錯誤，臨床試驗是過去式，現在已經是正式核准階段，不需要參與試驗才能取得。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, the author states that the drug is a 'tool to support' healthy habits. What does 'tool' mean in this context?

options.A: A physical instrument used for medical surgery.
options.B: A means or instrument used to achieve a particular goal.
options.C: A person who is easily manipulated by others.
options.D: A piece of software designed for data analysis.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「tool」並非指手術刀具（選項 A），也不是指被操縱的人（選項 C）或軟體（選項 D）。它是比喻用法，意指該藥物是一種「輔助手段」或「工具」，用來幫助患者達成減重目標，但必須配合生活習慣的改變。這呼應了文中強調藥物不能取代健康飲食與運動的觀點。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the new Wegovy pill?

options.A: The UK has become the leading country in developing weight-loss technology.
options.B: The new pill is a guaranteed way to lose weight without changing one's lifestyle.
options.C: The pill is a convenient new option, but it requires medical supervision and lifestyle changes to be effective.
options.D: The NHS has officially recommended the pill as the most effective treatment for obesity.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心在於報導 Wegovy 藥丸在英國獲得核准的訊息，同時平衡地提醒讀者該藥物的便利性與其潛在的副作用及限制。文章強調這並非「神奇藥丸」（magic solution），而是需要配合健康生活方式的輔助工具。選項 A 過度誇大英國的地位；選項 B 與文中強調「生活習慣改變」的觀點完全相反；選項 D 錯誤，因為 NHS 目前尚未將其納入推薦清單。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
